title: Swansea police recover 5 guns from home after report that second grader brought weapon to school
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Swansea police recovered five guns from a home after investigating reports of a second grader bringing a gun to school.
The guns were unregistered and improperly stored, with no valid Firearms Identification Card held by residents.
Charges are pending against two residents, and increased police presence at the school is expected as a precaution.

title: He grew up in R.I., named himself after Ted Williams, and is now drawing comparisons to Elvis and Roy Orbison. Meet Tyler Ballgame.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Tyler Ballgame, a Rhode Island native and former Berklee student, debuts album 'For the First Time, Again'.
Ballgame, who renamed himself from Tyler Perry, is known for his genre-defying style and commanding stage presence.
His career gained momentum after moving to L.A., resulting in a record deal and a North American tour.

title: For $225k, you could live in America’s oldest shopping mall
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Arcade in Providence, built in 1828, is the oldest enclosed shopping mall in the U.S., housing 48 residences.
Unit 314, a 265-square-foot microloft at The Arcade, is listed for $225,000 with a monthly HOA fee of $198.29.
The unit features a kitchenette, a bedroom nook, and offers potential for student housing or short-term rental usage.

title: Jayson Tatum is ‘not thrilled’ about first trip to Madison Square Garden since injury
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is set to return to Madison Square Garden for the first time since rupturing his Achilles tendon there.
Tatum is not thrilled about the return due to the traumatic experience but committed to playing scheduled games.
Tatum's performance has improved post-recovery, averaging 10 rebounds per game for the first time in his career.

title: 5 Bruins thoughts on a winless road trip, James Hagens, and playoff matchups
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins went 0-2-2 on a winless four-game road trip, including an overtime loss to the Hurricanes.
The Bruins have a 99% chance of making the playoffs, potentially clinching with losses from other teams on Thursday.
James Hagens is a potential call-up for the Bruins, aimed at improving their struggling offensive lineup.

title: When she was 11, Vanna Howard was in a refugee camp. Now she’s the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Vanna Howard was sworn in as the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S. on March 18, 2026.
Howard represents Massachusetts' 1st Middlesex District, focusing on housing, health care, and immigrant communities.
Arriving as a refugee from Cambodia, Howard emphasizes listening and serving diverse communities in Lowell and beyond.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 8)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: In Abington, a one-family Cape Cod home on Doris Drive sold for $515,000 and a conventional home on Brockton Ave for $550,000.
A colonial-style home in Acton on Charter Road, built in 1950, was sold for $1,205,000, featuring 4 bedrooms and 3 baths.
In Boston, a condo at 1 Dalton St. sold for $14 million, the highest in the latest reported real estate transactions.

title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville's Newbury Street location often has lines due to its unique 'one size fits all' clothing policy.
Blank Street coffee shop employs line security at its Boylston Street location due to frequent long lines.
Brick Street Bagels in South Boston is known for its morning crowds and strong social media presence.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for injections in her two Massachusetts medical spas.
Fadanelli, unlicensed to perform injections, imported fake Botox and dermal fillers from China and Brazil.
She faces up to 20 years in prison and a $250,000 fine for importing and selling counterfeit products.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Police fatally shot 29-year-old Jacob Graves near Northeastern University after he attacked officers and EMS responders with a sword-like weapon.
Graves, experiencing a mental health crisis, believed armed individuals were threatening him, leading to his distress call to 911.
Several recent violent incidents near Northeastern's campus have prompted protests over perceived inadequate communication from university administration.
